message:
fix: two more bugs from round-5 audit (CARGO_TARGET_DIR + SLSA git context)

1. upgrade_verify.sh: respect CARGO_TARGET_DIR when locating the
   built Rust binary.  _upgrade_build_tool hardcoded
   $repo_dir/target/release/$tool_name and fell through with an
   empty bin_path whenever cargo wrote to a different target
   directory.  On systems that set CARGO_TARGET_DIR globally (this
   VPS uses /data/tmp/cargo-target), the build always succeeded but
   the verify step always reported "Failed to build" — the entire
   Rust path was silently dead.  Now we ask cargo where the target
   directory actually is (via `cargo metadata`) and fall back to
   $CARGO_TARGET_DIR / $repo_dir/target in that order.  Also added
   `--bin "$tool_name"` so multi-binary crates produce the right
   binary, and `cd "$repo_dir"` for the Go path so module mode
   resolves regardless of caller PWD.

2. slsa.sh: stop reading git provenance from the wrong directory.
   slsa_generate ran `git rev-parse HEAD` from the dsr process's
   PWD.  When invoked from anywhere other than the source repo
   (e.g. a build directory or /tmp), the provenance silently
   shipped with empty git_repo / git_commit — defeating the point
   of SLSA provenance.  Now we accept --repo-path explicitly, and
   otherwise probe the artifact's directory then PWD, only using
   a candidate that's actually inside a git repo.  Warns loudly
   when no repo can be located so users notice instead of getting
   silently-empty source attribution.  The `dsr slsa generate`
   wrapper exposes the new flag with help text.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -7634,8 +7634,10 @@ SUBCOMMANDS:
     verify      Verify SLSA provenance matches artifact digest
 
 OPTIONS:
-    --builder, -b <id>  Builder identity URL
-    --output, -o <file> Output file (default: <artifact>.intoto.jsonl)
+    --builder, -b <id>   Builder identity URL
+    --output, -o <file>  Output file (default: <artifact>.intoto.jsonl)
+    --repo-path <dir>    Source repo to read git provenance from
+                         (default: artifact's directory, then PWD)
 
 DESCRIPTION:
     Generates SLSA v1 provenance (in-toto Statement) for release artifacts.
@@ -7645,11 +7647,12 @@ DESCRIPTION:
     - Artifact name and SHA256 digest
     - Builder identity
     - Build timestamps
-    - Git source info (if available)
+    - Git source info (resolved via --repo-path / artifact dir / PWD)
 
 EXAMPLES:
     dsr slsa generate ./build/ntm-linux-amd64
     dsr slsa generate ./build/ntm-darwin-arm64 --builder "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/dsr"
+    dsr slsa generate /tmp/out/ntm-linux-amd64 --repo-path /data/projects/ntm
     dsr slsa verify ./build/ntm-linux-amd64
 
 EXIT CODES:
@@ -7671,11 +7674,13 @@ _slsa_generate() {
     local artifact=""
     local builder_id="$SLSA_BUILDER_ID"
     local output=""
+    local repo_path=""
 
     while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
         case "$1" in
             --builder|-b) builder_id="$2"; shift 2 ;;
             --output|-o) output="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+            --repo-path) repo_path="$2"; shift 2 ;;
             -*) log_error "Unknown option: $1"; return 4 ;;
             *) artifact="$1"; shift ;;
         esac
@@ -7691,6 +7696,7 @@ _slsa_generate() {
 
     local -a args=()
     [[ -n "$output" ]] && args+=("--output" "$output")
+    [[ -n "$repo_path" ]] && args+=("--repo-path" "$repo_path")
 
     if result=$(slsa_generate "$artifact" --builder "$builder_id" "${args[@]}" 2>&1); then
         local duration=$(($(date +%s) - start_time))

@@ -58,13 +58,15 @@ slsa_generate() {
     local output=""
     local build_type="dsr-local"
     local invocation_id=""
+    local repo_path=""
 
     while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
         case "$1" in
             --builder|-b) builder_id="$2"; shift 2 ;;
             --output|-o) output="$2"; shift 2 ;;
             --build-type) build_type="$2"; shift 2 ;;
             --invocation-id) invocation_id="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+            --repo-path) repo_path="$2"; shift 2 ;;
             *) shift ;;
         esac
     done
@@ -109,12 +111,35 @@ slsa_generate() {
         invocation_id="$(hostname)-$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)-$$"
     fi
 
-    # Get git info if available
+    # Get git info if available.
+    # Resolution order for source-of-truth repo:
+    #   1) explicit --repo-path argument
+    #   2) directory containing the artifact (for build-output workflows)
+    #   3) current working directory (legacy behaviour)
+    # Each candidate is probed with `git rev-parse --git-dir` so we
+    # only use a directory that's actually inside a git repo, and we
+    # warn loudly when no repo can be located — provenance with empty
+    # source info is misleading.
     local git_repo="" git_commit="" git_ref=""
     if command -v git &>/dev/null; then
-        git_repo=$(git config --get remote.origin.url 2>/dev/null || echo "")
-        git_commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "")
-        git_ref=$(git symbolic-ref -q HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "")
+        local git_search_dirs=()
+        [[ -n "$repo_path" ]] && git_search_dirs+=("$repo_path")
+        git_search_dirs+=("$(dirname "$artifact")" "$PWD")
+        local git_dir=""
+        for candidate in "${git_search_dirs[@]}"; do
+            [[ -z "$candidate" || ! -d "$candidate" ]] && continue
+            if git -C "$candidate" rev-parse --git-dir &>/dev/null; then
+                git_dir="$candidate"
+                break
+            fi
+        done
+        if [[ -n "$git_dir" ]]; then
+            git_repo=$(git -C "$git_dir" config --get remote.origin.url 2>/dev/null || echo "")
+            git_commit=$(git -C "$git_dir" rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "")
+            git_ref=$(git -C "$git_dir" symbolic-ref -q HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "")
+        else
+            log_warn "No git repository found for $artifact (tried --repo-path, artifact dir, PWD); provenance source info will be empty"
+        fi
     fi
 
     # Build the in-toto statement with SLSA v1 provenance predicate

@@ -388,20 +388,37 @@ _upgrade_build_tool() {
     local bin_path="$tmpdir/$tool_name"
 
     if [[ -f "$repo_dir/go.mod" ]]; then
-        # Go project
+        # Go project.  cd into the module so module-mode resolves
+        # correctly regardless of the caller's PWD; the subshell
+        # contains the cd so the rest of the script is unaffected.
         log_debug "Building Go project..."
         if [[ -d "$repo_dir/cmd/$tool_name" ]]; then
-            go build -o "$bin_path" "$repo_dir/cmd/$tool_name" 2>/dev/null
+            ( cd "$repo_dir" && go build -o "$bin_path" "./cmd/$tool_name" ) 2>/dev/null
         elif [[ -f "$repo_dir/main.go" ]]; then
-            go build -o "$bin_path" "$repo_dir" 2>/dev/null
+            ( cd "$repo_dir" && go build -o "$bin_path" . ) 2>/dev/null
         fi
     elif [[ -f "$repo_dir/Cargo.toml" ]]; then
-        # Rust project
+        # Rust project.  Use --bin so we get the right binary in
+        # multi-binary crates, then ask cargo where target_directory
+        # actually is so this works under CARGO_TARGET_DIR overrides
+        # (e.g. /data/tmp/cargo-target on this VPS).  Previously we
+        # hardcoded $repo_dir/target/release/$tool_name, which
+        # silently failed whenever CARGO_TARGET_DIR was set.
         log_debug "Building Rust project..."
-        cargo build --release --manifest-path "$repo_dir/Cargo.toml" 2>/dev/null
-        local target_path="$repo_dir/target/release/$tool_name"
-        if [[ -f "$target_path" ]]; then
-            cp "$target_path" "$bin_path"
+        if cargo build --release \
+                --manifest-path "$repo_dir/Cargo.toml" \
+                --bin "$tool_name" 2>/dev/null; then
+            local target_dir target_path
+            if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
+                target_dir=$(cargo metadata --no-deps --format-version=1 \
+                                --manifest-path "$repo_dir/Cargo.toml" 2>/dev/null \
+                                | jq -r '.target_directory // empty' 2>/dev/null)
+            fi
+            [[ -z "$target_dir" ]] && target_dir="${CARGO_TARGET_DIR:-$repo_dir/target}"
+            target_path="$target_dir/release/$tool_name"
+            if [[ -f "$target_path" ]]; then
+                cp "$target_path" "$bin_path"
+            fi
         fi
     fi
